1.11a: Contact methods: Secure Link via LMS Conveyancer Zone dashboard, telephone 03333 990055, or email conveyancing@atombank.co.uk.
1.11b: Contact methods: Secure Link via LMS Conveyancer Zone dashboard, telephone 03333 990055, or email conveyancing@atombank.co.uk.
1.7: Borrower and mortgagor must be the same entity.
Lending is not permitted where borrower and mortgagor are different.
1.14: Firm may act if person dealing or family is seller provided: firm is a partnership, a separate unrelated partner acts, no conflict of interest exists, and professional guidelines are followed.
1.15: Firm may act if person dealing or family is borrower provided: firm is a partnership, a separate unrelated partner acts, no conflict of interest exists, and professional guidelines are followed.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's legal representative details is not required.
3.1.4: Notify the lender only if the seller is unrepresented or if identity checks on the seller's representative are unsatisfactory.
3.1.5: Accepted Identity (List A): Electronic AML printout from Credit Reference Agency, National Identity Card, Northern Ireland Electoral Office Identity Card.
Accepted Identity (List B): Old style full UK driving licence, state/local benefit entitlement evidence (housing, council tax, pension, grant), court appointment instrument.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's legal representative details is not required.
4.5b: Certificates of Title must be submitted electronically via LMS Conveyancer Zone Portal.
Confirm borrower has accepted most recent mortgage offer in Atom App before submission.
Minimum notice for Certificate of Title is 3 working days.
4.1: Physical valuation reports are automatically provided to the STARS system record.
Online valuations (AVM) do not generate a report for the conveyancer.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are prohibited.
Sub-sales and back-to-back transactions are generally not acceptable.
Report all other instances where seller is not the owner to the Mortgage Team.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are only required if specified in the mortgage offer, requested by the valuer, or if they indicate a risk to security.
5.4.5: Personal searches accepted if agent: subscribes to the search code, is PCCB monitored, is COPSO registered, and has adequate PI insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for purchases and remortgages at the conveyancer's risk.
Conveyancer must be able to provide an unqualified Certificate of Title using the insurance.
5.5.3b: Originals not required; keep copy on file.
Notification required if property was converted in the last 10 years.
5.5.3c: Originals not required; keep copy on file.
Notification required if property was converted in the last 10 years.
5.5.3d: Certificate of lawful use is only required for commercial-to-residential conversions.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted if element does not exceed 15% and adequate rights of support and enforceable repairing covenants exist.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not accepted.
Acceptable exceptions: Leasehold interest with share of freehold, Tyneside flats, or where all other flats in block are leasehold.
5.7.1c: Reporting is not required if conditions in 5.7 are met.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into 4 or fewer flats where borrower owns the freehold and occupies a flat while others are on long leases are not acceptable.
5.8.5: Lender accepts arrangements where borrower owns the freehold reversion of the other flat in a 2-flat building and vice versa.
A charge is required over both the leasehold and freehold reversion titles.
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from third parties require a standard gift declaration confirming the gift is unconditional, non-refundable, and carries no interest in the property.
Conveyancer must confirm source of funds.
Gifted deposits from non-family members must be reported.
Existing charges for gifted deposits must be redeemed on remortgage completion.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent or insolvent landlords is accepted if comprehensive and enables an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 80 years at commencement and 50 years at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Ground rent cap: 0.2% of property value.
Ground rent review frequency: 10 years or greater.
RPI-linked ground rent escalation is acceptable.
Leases with potential AST status under Housing Act 1988 must be varied or covered by suitable indemnity insurance.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if it allows for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.14.12: Ground rent and service charge receipts should be retained on file, not sent to the lender.
5.14.13: Evidence of notice service should be sent to the borrower and kept with title deeds, not sent to the lender.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should not be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms should be retained on file, not sent.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and Articles of Association should not be sent.
5.17.5a: Original Power of Attorney and specimen signature must be sent for inspection before completion if the account is operated under POA.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of POA is to be held on file, not sent.
5.20.1: Lender will not lend if solar panels are subject to a lease arrangement for the roof/roof space.
Solar panels are acceptable if funded by borrower or additional borrowing without a lease; must have planning permission.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed via 1.11a contact details.
6.2.1: All discrepancies in property description must be reported via 1.11a contact details.
6.4.4: Report incentives if cash exceeds: the valuer's stated amount, the offer amount, or 5% of the purchase price.
Exempt incentives: white goods, carpets, curtains, kitchen/bathroom/electrical upgrades, turf, and landscaping.
CML Disclosure of Incentives Form must be completed.
6.6.1: Notify the lender only if the mortgage offer does not already reflect that the property is let or to be let.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements are to be held on file unless the offer states otherwise.
6.6.4: Lender does not lend on properties subject to multiple occupancy (HMOs).
6.7.1: Acceptable Warranty Schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans, Building Zone, LABC, Checkmate, Construction Register, HAPM, Advantage, Aedis, ICW, Q Assure, One Guarantee, Global Homes, ARK Residential, ABC+, CADIS.
Professional Consultant's Certificate (PCC) is accepted from RIBA or RICS corporate members with PI insurance.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be sent.
6.7.4: New builds or properties built in last 10 years without a warranty are unacceptable.
Converted properties without a warranty are accepted if they have full building regulations, planning, and a completion certificate.
Full structural conversions require a warranty as per 6.7.1.
6.7.6: Professional Consultant’s Certificate must be sent to the lender.
6.10.2: Retention policy: No retentions permitted; offers based on current value with full funds released on completion.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required for all occupants aged 17 or over and anyone with a financial interest.
Exemption: Full-time students living away from home do not require a letter of consent.
8.1: Advice to third parties is permitted if no conflict of interest exists and documents are not open to challenge.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent with the title documents package.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be at least the open market value as stated in the valuation report.
10.2a: Funds paid electronically only; cheques not accepted.
Completion funds and cashback are sent as a single payment.
Cashback must not be released to the customer until after completion.
10.2b: Minimum 3 working days notice required for the Certificate of Title.
10.3: Deductions include CHAPS fee and fees specified in the mortgage offer.
10.7: Return funds electronically via Faster Payments or CHAPS within 1 day.
Return Account: Sort 60-00-01, Account 48540870 (Atom Bank PLC – Phoebus Cash).
Reference must include borrower name and account number.
10.10: Funds can be held for 1 day.
Can be held up to 3 working days if the lender is notified and a revised CoT is provided.
10.11: Interest is charged from the date of completion at the rate specified in the mortgage offer.
14.2.1: Do not send physical title deeds.
Upload required documents to the LMS STARS system.
14.2.2: Required post-completion documents: Priority Notice, Confirmation of Application to Register, Official Copy Register, Original Mortgage Deed.
16.1.1: Lender requires direct authority from the customer before releasing account information.
16.3.7b: Standard attestation: Executed as a deed by ….. as attorney for and on behalf of Atom Bank plc in the presence of ...
16.3.4: Transfer of Equity documents do not need to be sent as the lender will always take a new charge.
16.5.3a: Send Deed of Variation to: Atom Bank for Intermediaries, PO Box 105, Blyth, NE24 9DU.
16.5.3b: Send Deed of Rectification to: Atom Bank for Intermediaries, PO Box 105, Blyth, NE24 9DU.
16.5.3c: Send Deed of Easement to: Atom Bank for Intermediaries, PO Box 105, Blyth, NE24 9DU.
16.5.3d: Send Option Agreements to: Atom Bank for Intermediaries, PO Box 105, Blyth, NE24 9DU.
17.1.1: Redemption statements must be requested via the Atom Bank website.
Statements are delivered to the customer App document vault for sharing with the solicitor.
17.2.1b: Discharge is processed directly with the Land Registry by the lender.
